biteOfDay: Low-flow Taneycomo morning (~20 cfs overnight into 9 AM) then heavy afternoon generation (~11.6k cfs) — fish early, then switch to drifting when water moves. Table Rock near full pool for dawn topwater.

tableRock.temp: Verify locally (typically low-mid 80s F surface mid-summer)
tableRock.level: 916.91 ft MSL at 08:00 CT 07/21; 916.85 ft by 21:00 CT (USACE TRZM7) — near full power pool 917.0
tableRock.clarity: Clear near dam (typical); verify arms locally

taneycomo.temp: Verify locally (~50 F regime common below dam with generation)
taneycomo.generation: 07/21 USACE: ~20 cfs 02:00-09:00 CT; ramp midday (~3-6k cfs); heavy ~11,500-11,700 cfs 15:00-21:00 CT. Call SWPA 866-494-1993 to confirm live.

species.name: Bass
species.technique: Topwater at dawn; deep spoon, shakey head, deep crank mid-day

species.name: Walleye
species.technique: Crankbaits, live bait (carried mid-summer pattern — not freshly guide-verified 07-21)

species.name: Crappie
species.depth: Deep timber & brush piles (James River/Long Creek arms)
species.technique: Small white or yellow jigs under deep-water docks

species.name: Trout
species.technique: Low-flow morning window; pink worms/jigs/midges; drift when generation heavy (Trophy Area nightcrawlers)

species.name: Bluegill
species.technique: Worms, small poppers — great for kids (mid-summer pattern carry)

species.name: Catfish
species.depth: Indian Point & near dam, shallows after dark
species.technique: Stink bait, hot dogs, cut/frozen bait — evening/night

tip: Fish early. Table Rock topwater at sunrise. On Taneycomo, use the near-idle morning flows then drift when afternoon generation hits ~11.6k cfs. Confirm generation: SWPA 866-494-1993. Deadline-guard carry from 2026-07-21.
